methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Transportation. Range: $270M - $1.8B.

data.narrative:
Arc Boats, a Series B-stage transportation company founded by Mitch Lee and Ryan Cook, has raised $100M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 8 private transportation companies—including Einride (Series C, $1.8B), Harbinger (Series C, $500M), and REGENT Craft (Series A, $270M)—with no direct public comparables available.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $100M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $190M. Peer-median analysis across the 3 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage peers produces a $500M median valuation, with a range of $270M (low) to $1.8B (high). The peer-median method reflects broader market positioning but includes stage dispersion: two Series C peers skew the median upward relative to Arc's current Series B status.

Triangulating across both methods produces a valuation range of $162M (low) to $575M (high), with a median of $345M. The gap between methods reflects uncertainty in stage-adjusted peer comparability. Stage-progression anchors conservatively to capital deployed; peer-median captures market valuations but spans stage discontinuity. Key uncertainties include exact revenue and unit economics relative to peers, execution risk at Series B scale, and whether the peer set adequately reflects Arc's specific market position and growth trajectory.
